title: Running Kube in Podman is difficult or impossible.
body:
### Feature request description

We used the latest podman version, pulled directly from the website (same for podman-compose) on Ubuntu.  When starting "kind" inside a podman container, it was not possible for us to fix all permission problems: 

- The most persistent problem was the failure of the rootless Podman environment to initialize correctly for the vscode user. This manifested as a recurring Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1. This indicated that even with the correct packages (uidmap) and configurations (/etc/subuid), the nested container was being prevented from creating the user namespaces essential for rootless operation, likely due to a restriction on the host machine.

- Configuration Conflicts: Our debugging process was complicated by multiple configuration issues that created circular errors:
1.  Corrupted Storage State: Failed attempts left behind a corrupted storage directory (~/.local/share/containers/storage) that would override new, correct configurations, leading to the User-selected graph driver "vfs" overwritten by graph driver "overlay" from database error.

Missing Host Dependencies: Even after solving the driver issues, kind failed because the minimal devcontainer was missing host-level dependencies that kind expects to mount into its node containers, such as the /lib/modules directory.  This may not be a podman problem.  

We also noted that the devcontainers package when setting the driver to podman still made references to docker that made it impossible to use Cursor extensions (I filed a bug for that under devcontainers, because it's not a podman issue).  

Any advice on how to get past these difficulties is much appreciated - we think it would be wonderful if the complex podman in podman configurations became as easy as they are in Docker.  

Many thanks for the advice.

### Suggest potential solution

A clear and concise description of what you want to happen.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

A clear and concise description of any alternative solutions or features you've considered.

### Additional context

Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
